texts.content_text100: Hello, my name is Mariam Mosmotei. I am the executive director of the Center for European Constitutional Law. And in this uh short video, we are going to address the issue of protection of children's personal data and ethical responsibilities of uh lawyers. Before I start with um what I want to share with you, I would like to make a quick note that this uh video is made in the context of the foster project which is an EU funded initiative with a focus on a childfriendly justice uh system which is respectful of the rights and well-being of children uh victims of crime and the project aims to build the capacity of lawyers by providing train training on children's rights on the legal framework for the rights of
texts.content_text101: um uh victims of crime, traumainformed approaches and victim sensitive um communication. So when thinking about children's data protection among a zillion other things which are important when it comes to children in the criminal justice system, one would wonder why is this important and why do we need to uh learn something about that? I think it's two or three main reasons why this is an important issue. The first one is that the protection of privacy and personal data is not anymore something just nice to have. It's not an add-on. It's a fundamental right. It is a fundamental right that is in the treaties that is in um the charter EU charter fundamental right. So it's not it's no longer just um discretionary whe whether to do anything about it. It is a legal obligation. The second aspect of that is that when it comes to children um because of their vulnerability um data protection and privacy become even more important. Children might not be able to identify breaches, might not be able to react to breaches uh and protect themselves. So might uh find themselves exposed to victimization, to secondary victimization or to uh further uh abuse. So all of these reasons make it mandatory to uh take a close look and make sure that we comply with the obligations in relation to uh children's uh rights. And I would just like to stress that there's a two facets in this um uh issue. The first one is the legal one which is that we have an obligation to protect that and the second one is that there is an ethical dimension that has to do with the role of lawyers in criminal uh justice uh systems.
texts.content_text102: So looking at the rules, what kind of rules are in place in relation to children's uh protection of uh privacy and and data and there are a number of uh provisions that we can rely on starting with the convention the rights of the child and article 16 which creates a right for children to the protection of their privacy. Um so this provision speaks to the children themselves and gives them a right to be protected and to react to any violations of their uh privacy. Um then we have of course the very famous data um uh GDPR. So so the general um data protection um uh regulation in the EU and there are a number of provisions there like 58 or 23 which um include specific reference um um to children and the protection of data of related to children. And we also have a specific directive which is directive 2016 800 which relates to procedural safeguards for children who are suspects or accused persons in criminal  proceedings. So this is important because it is relevant for children um it sets minimum standards um um uh for children who are in the criminal justice system. So it addresses member states not the children themselves but it requires them to bring in place provisions for in camera hearings for example or confidentiality or non-disclosure or um uh protection of information or dissemination of information uh and so on. Um we also have some soft law instruments not in the EU but in the Council of Europe uh ecosystem. Uh soft law are not binding. So no one is bound by them legally bound by them but they offer guidelines and off and and very useful uh instructions on how to address different topics.
texts.content_text103: Coming now specifically to the obligations of uh lawyers and we already mentioned that there are legal obligations and there are also ethical obligations and of course lawyers are bound by their professional codes of conduct um um which uh make them which require from them respect for confidentiality and a number of other things. So I would urge you to take a good look, a deep  dive into your professional codes of conduct and see what would be relevant to data protection and specifically to data protection of children in that um uh context within uh the general obligations of confidentiality and protection of their clients interests. Lawyers would have to um specify these in relation to children in particular. There are also some helpful instruments and guidelines and guides and manuals which are available and can help you unpack these kind of ethical uh obligations within your jurisdiction within your own uh legal order.
texts.content_text104: So in order to conclude um I would like to stress once again the protection of fun privacy is a fundamental rights issue. So it's not optional it's mandatory and it requires special guarantees from states from criminal justice systems but also from uh lawyers for the protection of children. Lawyers in particular need to be aware of privacy and data um protection issues, comply with the existing rules, be able to translate this into a context that relates to children and ensure a child-friendly uh and a child- centered approach in their practice and of course identify in a timely manner and know what to do and how to react to breaches of privacy and data protection. This is all for me for now.
texts.content_text105: I hope this has been useful and thank you very much for listening.
